children.text: Endometriosis is a complex and misunderstood condition that affects an estimated 1 in 10 people during their reproductive years. Despite being relatively common, there’s still a shroud of mystery surrounding it, which often leads to misdiagnosis and years of silent struggle. This article aims to shed more light on the complexities of this complicated disease, its immense impact on health and wellness, and the ongoing journey to understand it better.

editorialText.title: What is endometriosis?

children.text: Endometriosis

is a condition in which cells similar to, but not identical to the lining of the uterus are located of the uterus. Common areas include the uterosacral ligaments, pelvic side walls, ovaries, and fallopian tubes. Endometriosis lesions release inflammatory factors that can cause pain and adhesions in the areas they are located. Endometriosis can cause acute pain, chronic pain, and infertility. Adenomyosis, a condition often found in people with endometriosis, can cause cramping pain. (Endometriosis doesn't really shed like endometrium dose, it's quite different in many ways. It doesn't implant (like retrograde menstruation suggests).
As an aside... It is likely something people are born with, as it is found in fetuses and is located on areas outside of the pelvis in some people.
The cause of endometriosis is unknown, however the limited research conducted on this condition has led to several working theories:
Retrograde menstruation:
Menstrual blood containing endometrial cells flows backward through the fallopian tubes and into the pelvic cavity where it deposits outside the uterus, implants, and grows.
Cellular metaplasia:
Cells outside the uterus change into endometrial-like cells and spread throughout the body via blood or lymphatic vessels.
Genetics:
People are more likely to experience endometriosis if a family member has the disorder.
Immune-system complications:
When the body’s natural defense system breaks down, endometriosis is more likely to occur.

editorialText.title: The truth about endometriosis symptoms

children.text: The hallmark symptom of endometriosis is pelvic pain, usually associated with menstrual periods. While many people experience pain, pressure, or cramps during their cycles, endometriosis sufferers report pain that’s far worse than usual. Making matters more complicated (and making its diagnosis more difficult), symptoms of endometriosis aren’t limited to period pain, and the severity of pain isn’t a reliable indicator of the extent of the condition. Some people with mild endometriosis have intense pain; others with advanced disease might have mild symptoms.
Other Symptoms of Endometriosis:
Pain during sex
Pain with bowel movements or urination
Infertility
Excessive bleeding during or between periods
Anxiety, depression, and fatigue
Bloating, constipation, diarrhea, and nausea
Experiencing symptoms of endometriosis? Join Tia's membership and schedule an appointment to discuss your options at the clinic nearest you!
Located in New York, Phoenix, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

editorialText.title: The dilemma of diagnosing endometriosis

children.text: Diagnosing endometriosis is a notorious challenge. Also, because many symptoms of endometriosis overlap with other conditions, it’s commonly misdiagnosed as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), pelvic inflammatory disease (PID), or ovarian cysts among other things. In reality, the only definitive way to diagnose endometriosis is through laparoscopic surgery, a minimally-invasive surgical procedure where a camera is inserted into the pelvis via a small incision. This allows a doctor to “explore” the pelvis and directly view endometrial tissue outside the uterus. Short of this, most doctors consider endometriosis to be a diagnosis-of-exclusion: Once they have ruled out other conditions with similar presentations, they diagnose endometriosis in their absence.

editorialText.title: A tailored approach to treating endometriosis

children.text: Currently, there is
no known way to prevent endometriosis or cure the condition,
so treatment is essentially aimed at controlling its symptoms:
Pain medication:
For mild symptoms, healthcare providers may recommend over-the-counter pain relievers like ibuprofen or naproxen.
Continuous hormonal therapy:

Medications containing estrogen and progestin (birth control pills, vaginal rings, patches) can be used to control periods and suppress the activity of endometriosis. All hormonal meds appear to be most effective when used in a continuous fashion, which means skipping the hormone-free week when you’d normally have your period. Some research indicates that continuous progestin-only hormonal therapy (pills, IUDs) is even more effective at controlling endometriosis symptoms than combination hormonal therapy.
Danazol:
A synthetic androgen (male hormone) that inactivates endometriosis implants and lowers estrogen levels.
Lupron:
Given as a shot every 1-3 months, Lupron works by stopping the hormonal signals that tell the ovaries to make estrogen, which can make endometriosis inactive.
Exploratory laparoscopic surgery:
For people wanting to get pregnant, surgery to remove as much endometriosis as possible while preserving the uterus and ovaries may reduce symptoms and increase fertility.
Hysterectomy:
In severe cases and when no future pregnancy is desired, surgery to remove the uterus and ovaries is the most effective and definitive treatment available.

editorialText.title: How does endometriosis impact fertility?

children.text: Endometriosis can significantly impact fertility in several ways:
Inflammation and scarring:

Endometriosis can cause inflammation and scarring in the pelvic region, which can affect the functioning of the ovaries, fallopian tubes, and uterus. This inflammation can lead to the formation of adhesions, which are bands of scar tissue that can block the fallopian tubes, preventing the egg from traveling from the ovary to the uterus.
Ovarian function:

Endometriosis can affect ovarian function, leading to the production of fewer eggs and a lower ovarian reserve (the number of eggs a woman has available for fertilization). This can make it more difficult to conceive.
Endometrial implantation:
In people with endometriosis, the endometrial tissue that grows outside the uterus can also affect the uterine lining, making it less receptive to implantation of a fertilized egg.
Inflammatory environment:
The inflammatory environment created by endometriosis can also affect the quality of the sperm and the ability of the sperm to fertilize the egg.
Hormonal imbalance:
Endometriosis is associated with hormonal imbalances, particularly elevated levels of estrogen. This can affect ovulation and the menstrual cycle, making it more difficult to conceive.
Tia has your back

Endometriosis is a multi-faceted, misunderstood condition that extends far beyond physical symptoms. Chronic pain can lead to emotional distress, depression, and anxiety. Infertility struggles can be mentally exhausting and emotionally taxing. And the uncertainty and unpredictability of symptoms often disrupts personal relationships and professional life. Awareness and education are essential to early diagnosis and effective management. It can also be useful to build a support system – friends, family, groups – to help navigate the journey. Remember, endometriosis is a common condition, and there’s no need to struggle in solitude or travel the road alone. If you’re experiencing endometriosis symptoms, talk to your healthcare provider and don’t be afraid to advocate for yourself.
At Tia, you will always be seen, heard, and taken seriously. Our team of clinical experts are experienced in diagnosing and treating women’s health issues like endometriosis, and our dedicated
endometriosis appointments
provide a safe, supportive space in which to explore symptoms and treatment options that are purposeful and personalized.
Struggling with endometriosis? Join Tia's membership and schedule an appointment to discuss your options at the clinic nearest you!
Located in New York, Phoenix, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

children.text: Endometriosis is a common condition that affects many people around the world. How common is it?
Endometriosis impacts
the lives of roughly 10% (190 million) of women during their reproductive years.

Endometriosis is a chronic disease that can cause a constellation of uncomfortable symptoms, including pain during periods, sex, and bowel movements, irregular bleeding, abdominal bloating, nausea, fatigue, anxiety, and depression. It is characterized by tissue similar to the lining of the uterus growing outside of it and implanting on other reproductive organs (fallopian tubes, ovaries, uterine ligaments),
Endometriosis can also
cause infertility

in 30-50% of people suffering from this condition. If you’re living with endometriosis and dreaming about starting or growing a family, you’re probably wondering how endometriosis might affect your pregnancy journey. If so, there’s good news ahead. Even if there is no absolute cure for endometriosis, there are many proven treatments aimed at controlling its symptoms, and many people with the condition become pregnant and have healthy pregnancies and babies.
How Endometriosis Impacts Fertility

Although the medical community has been conducting research and making educated guesses for years, there’s still a lot it doesn’t know about endometriosis, including its exact cause. But there’s something the medical community
does
know about the condition: people with endometriosis often have a more challenging time getting pregnant.
But at Tia, our team of women’s health experts is committed to helping our members manage their endometriosis with a comprehensive approach that ensures their whole health is taken into account.
Struggling with fertility? Become a Tia member and schedule an appointment at a clinic near you!
Located in New York, Scottsdale, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

For pregnancy to occur, a healthy egg must be released from an ovary and then travel through a fallopian tube to be fertilized by a healthy sperm. Next, the fertilized egg must travel to the uterus and, given the right conditions, attach itself to the wall to start developing.
Endometriosis, can make getting pregnant difficult and impact overall fertility in several ways:
Endometriosis can cause chronic inflammation of the pelvis and pelvic organs, which creates a harsh environment for ovulation, fertilization, and implantation.
Endometriosis can cause scar tissue that blocks the fallopian tubes and prevents an egg and sperm from uniting.
Endometriosis (especially if it exists on the ovaries) can affect the quality of eggs released by the ovaries. It can also damage eggs and sperm after their release.
Endometriosis can alter the environment in the uterus and prevent a fertilized egg from implanting successfully.
Endometriosis can create a hormonal imbalance that isn’t conducive to ovulation, fertilization, and implantation.
Endometriosis can alter the immune system in ways that affect pregnancy.
Endometriosis can damage reproductive organs and prevent them from working properly.
In general, inflammation seems to be at the core of
endometriosis
and the problems it causes. Still, it’s worth repeating:
Many people with mild to moderate endometriosis can still ovulate, conceive, and carry a healthy pregnancy to term.

The medical community also knows that endometriosis is a progressive disease that generally progresses over time, and this is why many healthcare professionals recommend trying to have children at the earliest signs and symptoms of endometriosis.
Endometriosis and Pregnancy

If you have endometriosis and are considering pregnancy, it's a good idea to start with a visit to your healthcare provider. They can offer guidance and treatments tailored to your specific situation, which might include managing endometriosis symptoms (NSAIDs, hormonal birth control methods, GnRH agonists), assessing fertility, and exploring fertility treatments.

In the early stages of endometriosis, you might be able to get pregnant without any medical intervention. Oftentimes, at this stage, controlling symptoms is enough to get past the hurdle of endometriosis.

If you’re finding it more difficult to conceive, your doctor might recommend a laparoscopy to surgically remove endometriosis. During this simple procedure, a medical professional inserts a camera through a small incision in the abdomen to assess your endometriosis lesions and remove as many as possible.
For people who are 35 + or diagnosed with a more advanced form of the condition, your doctor might recommend the fertility medication to help trigger ovulation.

Additionally, your provider may suggest intrauterine insemination (IUI) instead of surgery. During this treatment, when ovulation occurs, your physician will place sperm into the uterus with a thin tube with the goal of improving the chances for fertilization and implantation.
If this treatment is unsuccessful or you have late-stage endometriosis, your doctor will likely recommend assisted reproductive technologies (ART) such as in vitro fertilization (IVF).
There is more uplifting news:
Pregnancy can actually bring relief from endometriosis symptoms for some people.

During pregnancy, menstruation stops, which means the growth and shedding cycle that can make endometriosis worse is put on pause. Many people report a decrease in endometriosis-related pain during their pregnancy, and for some, this relief can continue after giving birth.
Pregancy is still possible with endometriosis

Every person is unique and every journey with endometriosis and pregnancy is personal. While endometriosis can make getting pregnant a challenge for some people, there is hope. Recently, there has been an uptick in attention to chronic conditions like endometriosis and the global toll these conditions can take.

Advances in treatments and a better understanding of endometriosis have made pregnancy a reality for many people who once thought it might be out of reach. Keeping an open dialogue with your doctor, staying up-to-date and informed, and leaning on your support network is paramount. But with patience, preparation, and proper care you, too, can likely navigate these challenges and start or grow your family with success.

If you’re experiencing symptoms of endometriosis and struggling to get a diagnosis and treatment plan, Tia is a good place to start. We take a holistic approach to women’s healthcare and offer a focused care for enodmetriosis to help discuss, diagnose, and manage this notoriously difficult-to-diagnose condition.
Trying to get pregnant with an endo diagnosis? Become a Tia member and schedule an appointment at a clinic near you!
Located in New York, Scottsdale, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story. We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly
one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary gynecologist and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our model ensures your gynecology issues are never treated in isolation. Our providers are trained to connect the dots between symptoms like irregular periods or painful sex and your overall physical, mental, and emotional health. This allows us to offer holistic, trauma-informed care that gets to the root cause of conditions like painful sex, irregular bleeding, chronic pelvic pain . These symptoms often connect back to hormones or stress. Our providers don’t just prescribe birth control; we help you find the option that best fits your body, values, and lifestyle.
This approach helps women get comprehensive care, covering all aspects of their well-being:
Integrated Prevention:
After joining Tia, members complete
life-saving screenings at rates 2–3x the national average
, ensuring your gynecologic health (like Pap tests and cervical cancer screenings) is proactively managed.
Seamless Specialty Referrals:
If you need specialty care, Tia partners with top health systems to coordinate your referral while remaining your primary care home.
Mental Health is GYN Health
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression
because we recognize the deep connection between chronic pain, stress, and mental health.

Why Gynecological and Sexual Health matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Gynecological symptoms aren't isolated; they’re often signals from the rest of your body. A dedicated care team helps connect the dots, understanding how stress, sleep, and overall health can influence things like your period or pelvic pain, sexual wellness, or irregular bleeding.
Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously.

You're not imagining it. For too long, women's pain—from endometriosis to chronic pelvic issues—has been dismissed and minimized. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the full picture of women's reproductive and sexual health who will listen actively and never brush off your concerns
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health.

Building a relationship with a trusted primary care team means catching problems early, from getting your cervical cancer screening (Pap test) on time to addressing changes in your reproductive health , ensuring you stay healthier for the long term.

"There is no question too personal, too complicated, or too embarrassing when it comes to your gynecologic and sexual health. Our primary role is to be a compassionate, non-judgmental partner in your total well-being."
Rebecca Reilly, DNP, FNP-C
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for Sexual and Gynecological health
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
Seamlessly transition between virtual consults for birth control and in-person procedures like IUD insertion.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Your primary care provider is always in sync with your GYN care, so you never have to repeat your history to get a Pap test, STI screen, or tackle a more complex condition.
✓ Seamless Specialty Access
Tia partners with leading health systems to ensure that if you need specialty care—for complex issues like advanced endometriosis—you are referred and supported while Tia remains your home base.
✓ Holistic/comprehensive care

✓ Care that grows with you

children.text: What are fibroids?

Uterine fibroids are non-cancerous growths that can form in the uterus during the years of menstruation. They are also known as leiomyomas or myomas. Unlike other types of cancer in the uterus, they are not linked to cancerous growth and do not typically develop into cancerous tumors.

Fibroids can vary in size and number. Some can be too small to see, while others can grow as big as a grapefruit or even larger. Large fibroids can change the shape of the uterus, causing it to expand. In severe cases, fibroids can grow so big that they occupy the pelvis or abdominal area, making a person's abdomen look swollen.
Many people
develop uterine fibroids at some point in their lives, but they often go unnoticed as they do not cause symptoms. They may be discovered during a pelvic exam or ultrasound.
Did you know?
About 20% to 80% of individuals with a uterus develop fibroids by age 50. They're most common in those in their 40s and 50s, but not everyone shows symptoms.
When to see a doctor about fibroids
The most common symptoms of uterine fibroids include:
Heavy menstrual bleeding or painful periods.
Longer or more frequent periods.
Pelvic pressure or pain.
Frequent urination or trouble urinating.
Growing stomach area.
Constipation.
Pain in the stomach area or lower back, or
pain during sex
What to expect during a fibroids appointment

Fibroid visits can be conducted both virtually and in clinic at Tia. This is a consultation based visit usually focused on a thorough review of current concerns, medical, family, medication, and pregnancy history and discussion or risk factors. If you choose to schedule an in-person appointment at a clinic, this visit may include a pelvic exam as large fibroids can sometimes be felt from the outside. Many times, additional information can be gained by ordering an ultrasound during the initial visit.
Potential treatment plans for fibroids
For those with disruptive symptoms like heavy bleeding and pain, treatment may include:
Medications:
These can treat heavy periods and temporarily shrink fibroids.
Non invasive:
A noninvasive treatment doesn't involve surgical cuts called incisions. It also doesn't involve tools being placed in the body.
Minimally invasive:
These procedures use no cuts or small cuts. They're linked with faster recovery times and fewer complications compared with traditional open surgery.
Traditional surgery:
Surgical intervention might be recommended depending on the severity of symptoms and size of the fibroids.
Not all diagnosis of fibroids will require medical intervention/treatment. Your Tia provider might recommend a more holistic approach to easing the symptoms of fibroids including:
Acupuncture:

Acupuncture can help decrease both the intensity and duration of menstrual cramps While there isn’t a one size fits all for acupuncture, we typically recommend coming in 1x weekly for 4-6 weeks for best results.
Turmeric:

There is research citing that adding in a Turmeric supplement can help to reduce the size and activity of endometriosis lesions. Turmeric helps to reduce inflammation and regulates blood flow to help reduce pain.
CBD:
CBD oral capsules or tincture can help with inflammation, cramping, and sleep.
Ginger:
Ingesting ginger during the first few days of menstruation may reduce the severity of your menstrual cramps.
Magnesium glycinate:
Helpful for reducing menstrual pain both preventatively and in the moment. It also helps to calm the mind, support healthy sleep, relax muscle tension, and encourage healthy cycles.
Need to see a doctor about your fibroids? Sign up for Tia's membership and schedule an appointment at the clinic nearest you!
Located in New York, Phoenix, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.
Uterine fibroids are a topic that deserves our attention. These non-cancerous growths in the uterus affect a significant number of women, with studies indicating that up to
70% of white women and 80% of black women
may develop fibroids
by the age of 50

. Despite their prevalence, fibroids are often overlooked and their impact on women's lives underestimated. That's why we're here to provide you with the information you need to understand and manage your health.

At Tia, we're committed to addressing healthcare inequality and bias. We strive to create a safe and inclusive space where you can find personalized, comprehensive care. Our mission is to empower you to advocate for your well-being, ensuring that you have the tools and knowledge to make informed decisions about your health. So, let's dive into the
Fibroid Files
and explore the symptoms, causes, and ways to take control of uterine fibroids together.

editorialText.title: What are Uterine Fibroids and What Causes Them

children.text: Uterine fibroids, also known as leiomyomas, are common benign tumors that develop in the muscular wall of the uterus. These growths are composed of smooth muscle tissue and fibrous connective tissue. While the exact cause of fibroids is not fully understood, researchers believe that a combination of genetic, hormonal, and environmental factors may play a role. Fibroids can vary in size, ranging from as
small as a seed to as large as a grapefruit
. They can be classified into different types based on their location within or on the uterus:
Intramural fibroids:
These fibroids develop within the muscular wall of the uterus and are the most common type.
Subserosal fibroids:
These fibroids grow outside the uterus, on the outer wall. They can cause pressure on surrounding organs and may lead to abdominal discomfort.
Submucosal fibroids:
These fibroids project into the inner cavity of the uterus and can cause heavy or prolonged menstrual bleeding, as well as fertility issues.
Pedunculated fibroids:
These fibroids are attached to the uterus by a stalk, known as a peduncle. They can either be subserosal or submucosal in nature.
So, what causes the development of uterine fibroids? While there is no definitive answer, several factors have been identified as potentially contributing to their formation:
Hormonal factors:

Estrogen and progesterone, two hormones involved in the regulation of the menstrual cycle, are believed to influence the growth of fibroids. These hormones can stimulate the proliferation of uterine cells and may contribute to the development of fibroids.
Genetic predisposition:

There is evidence to suggest that certain genetic mutations or alterations may increase the likelihood of developing fibroids. If you have a family history of fibroids, you may be at a higher risk of experiencing them yourself.
Racial and ethnic disparities:

Research has shown that fibroids are more prevalent among women of African descent, along with women from other racial and ethnic backgrounds, such as Caribbean and Hispanic. The reasons for these disparities are complex and still being explored.

While we continue to unravel the intricate mechanisms behind fibroid development, it's crucial to remember that every individual's experience with fibroids is unique. Understanding the different types of fibroids and the possible causes can serve as a foundation for navigating your healthcare journey with confidence and seeking the personalized care you deserve.

editorialText.title: Recognizing the Symptoms of Uterine Fibroids

children.text: While some women may experience no symptoms at all, others may find that fibroids significantly
impact their quality of life
. By recognizing these symptoms, you can better communicate with your healthcare provider and receive the support and treatment you need.
Menstrual changes:

Fibroids can cause heavy, prolonged menstrual bleeding, also known as menorrhagia. You may notice that your periods become longer, with increased clotting and the need to change sanitary products frequently. The excessive bleeding can lead to anemia, resulting in fatigue or weakness.
Pelvic pressure and pain:

As fibroids grow in size or number, they can cause a sense of pelvic pressure or fullness. This pressure can lead to discomfort or pain in the pelvis, lower back, or abdomen. Some women also experience pain during intercourse, known as dyspareunia.
Urinary symptoms:
Large fibroids can exert pressure on the bladder, leading to frequent urination, increased urgency, or even difficulty fully emptying the bladder.
Bowel symptoms:

Depending on the size and location of fibroids, they may put pressure on the rectum or intestines, resulting in constipation, bloating, or difficulty with bowel movements. In rare cases, fibroids can cause urinary or bowel obstruction.
Fertility concerns:
For some women, fibroids can interfere with fertility. The presence of submucosal fibroids, in particular, may affect the implantation of a fertilized egg or result in recurrent miscarriages.

These symptoms are nonspecific and can be associated with other conditions as well. If you experience any of these symptoms, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare provider who can properly evaluate your situation and provide personalized care. If you have questions or concerns about fibroids, check in with your provider during your yearly preventative check-up --
like the Tia Whole Health Exam

editorialText.title: Diagnosing and Treating Uterine Fibroids

children.text: When it comes to diagnosing and treating uterine fibroids, a personalized and comprehensive approach is key. Let's explore how fibroids are diagnosed and the treatment options available, empowering you to make informed decisions about your own health.
Diagnosing Uterine Fibroids
Medical history:

Your healthcare provider will begin by discussing your medical history and any symptoms you may be experiencing. Sharing your concerns openly and honestly will enable your provider to better understand your unique situation.
Physical examination:

During a physical examination, your healthcare provider may conduct a pelvic exam to assess the condition of your uterus. They will check for any abnormalities, such as the presence of fibroids, and evaluate their size and location.
Imaging tests:

In some cases, imaging tests may be necessary to confirm the presence of fibroids and examine their characteristics. These tests may include ultrasound, magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), or hysterosonography. These imaging modalities can provide detailed information about the size, number, and location of fibroids, helping your healthcare provider determine the most appropriate treatment approach.
Treatment Options for Uterine Fibroids
The type of treatment recommended will
depend on factors

such as the severity of your symptoms, your age, your desire for future fertility, and the size and location of the fibroids. Treatment decisions are highly individualized, and what works for one person may not be the best option for another. Your healthcare provider will work closely with you to develop a treatment plan tailored to your specific needs. Here are some common treatment options:
Watchful waiting:

If you have fibroids that are small, asymptomatic, or nearing menopause, your healthcare provider may suggest monitoring them without immediate intervention. Regular check-ups and discussions with your provider will ensure that any changes or symptoms are promptly addressed.
Medications:

Certain medications, such as hormonal birth control, can help manage the symptoms associated with fibroids. These medications work by regulating hormonal fluctuations and reducing heavy menstrual bleeding or pain. However, medications may not shrink or eliminate the fibroids themselves.
Minimally invasive procedures:

For those seeking symptom relief or fertility preservation, minimally invasive procedures may be recommended. These procedures aim to either remove the fibroids (myomectomy) or shrink them (uterine artery embolization). Minimally invasive procedures can offer potential benefits such as shorter recovery times, minimal scarring, and preservation of the uterus.
Surgical intervention:

In cases where fibroids are larger, multiple, or causing severe symptoms, surgical intervention may be necessary. A hysterectomy, which involves the removal of the uterus, may be recommended if fertility is no longer desired. However, there may be alternative surgical approaches that preserve the uterus, such as a myomectomy or robotic-assisted surgery. These options can be discussed with your healthcare provider.

The decision-making process regarding treatment for uterine fibroids should always be collaborative. Our healthcare providers at Tia are committed to providing members with the necessary information, support, and advocacy to ensure you receive the personalized, specific care that is right for you. By empowering yourself with knowledge and engaging in open communication, together we can navigate this journey with compassion and expertise.

editorialText.title: Lifestyle Changes and Self-Care for Uterine Fibroids

children.text: Taking care of yourself goes beyond medical treatments. At Tia, we understand the importance of holistic well-being and empowering our members with knowledge and tools to manage their health. While medical interventions may be necessary for uterine fibroids, there are also lifestyle changes and self-care practices that can support your overall well-being:
Healthy eating:

Nourishing your body with a balanced diet can have a positive impact on managing fibroid symptoms. Focus on incorporating nutrient-rich foods, such as fruits, vegetables, whole grains, lean proteins, and healthy fats. You may also consider reducing your consumption of processed foods, refined sugars, and caffeine, as these can potentially worsen symptoms for some individuals.
Managing stress:

Chronic stress can affect your hormonal balance and overall well-being. Finding healthy ways to manage stress is crucial. Explore stress-relieving techniques such as meditation, deep breathing exercises, yoga, or engaging in activities that bring you joy and relaxation.
Exercise and movement:

Regular physical activity can help improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote overall health. Engage in exercises that you enjoy and that align with your physical capabilities. Options may include walking, swimming, cycling, or gentle stretching.
Adequate sleep:
Prioritizing quality sleep is essential for your body's healing and overall well-being. Aim for 7-9 hours of uninterrupted sleep each night.
Support and community:

Living with uterine fibroids can sometimes feel overwhelming, but you don't have to face it alone. Seek out support groups or online communities where you can connect with others who share similar experiences. These communities can provide a space for sharing advice, gathering information, and finding emotional support.

Lifestyle changes and self-care practices can help manage symptoms, but they may not shrink or eliminate fibroids. If you continue to experience significant symptoms or have concerns, consult with your healthcare provider for personalized advice and appropriate medical interventions.

editorialText.title: Take Control of Uterine Fibroids

children.text: Understanding uterine fibroids is a crucial aspect of women's healthcare.  Armed with knowledge about fibroids, their symptoms, diagnosis, and treatment options, you can make informed decisions about your own health.

We encourage you to prioritize your well-being, advocate for your health needs, and be proactive in seeking medical attention if you have concerns about fibroids or any other aspect of your reproductive health.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story. We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly
one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary gynecologist and book your first visit.

Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our model ensures your gynecology issues are never treated in isolation. Our providers are trained to connect the dots between symptoms like irregular periods or painful sex and your overall physical, mental, and emotional health. This allows us to offer holistic, trauma-informed care that gets to the root cause of conditions like painful sex, irregular bleeding, chronic pelvic pain . These symptoms often connect back to hormones or stress. Our providers don’t just prescribe birth control; we help you find the option that best fits your body, values, and lifestyle.
This approach helps women get comprehensive care, covering all aspects of their well-being:
Integrated Prevention:
After joining Tia, members complete
life-saving screenings at rates 2–3x the national average
, ensuring your gynecologic health (like Pap tests and cervical cancer screenings) is proactively managed.
Seamless Specialty Referrals:
If you need specialty care, Tia partners with top health systems to coordinate your referral while remaining your primary care home.
Mental Health is GYN Health
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression
because we recognize the deep connection between chronic pain, stress, and mental health.

Why Gynecological and Sexual Health matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Gynecological symptoms aren't isolated; they’re often signals from the rest of your body. A dedicated care team helps connect the dots, understanding how stress, sleep, and overall health can influence things like your period or pelvic pain, sexual wellness, or irregular bleeding.
Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously.

You're not imagining it. For too long, women's pain—from endometriosis to chronic pelvic issues—has been dismissed and minimized. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the full picture of women's reproductive and sexual health who will listen actively and never brush off your concerns
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health.

Building a relationship with a trusted primary care team means catching problems early, from getting your cervical cancer screening (Pap test) on time to addressing changes in your reproductive health , ensuring you stay healthier for the long term.

"There is no question too personal, too complicated, or too embarrassing when it comes to your gynecologic and sexual health. Our primary role is to be a compassionate, non-judgmental partner in your total well-being."
Rebecca Reilly, DNP, FNP-C
, Market Medical Director

✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
Seamlessly transition between virtual consults for birth control and in-person procedures like IUD insertion.

Tia partners with leading health systems to ensure that if you need specialty care—for complex issues like advanced endometriosis—you are referred and supported while Tia remains your home base.
✓ Holistic/comprehensive care
We use both medication and integrative options (like acupuncture or pelvic floor therapy referrals) for chronic conditions like PCOS or painful sex, aiming to get to the root and give you options.
✓ Care that grows with you
We are your health home for every stage—from your first Pap test to navigating menopause and beyond.

children.text: Women’s Health Clinic in New York City

Why go to multiple doctors’ offices—primary care clinic here, gynecologist there—when you can have everything you need at one women’s health clinic in New York City? At Tia, we offer comprehensive, holistic support from compassionate primary care providers, gynecologists, mental health specialists, and wellness practitioners who are here for your whole health.

With locations in the Manhattan (Soho) and Brooklyn (Williamsburg), we’re making it easier for women across New York City to access personalized care that prioritizes your health. Whether you prefer in-person visits or virtual appointments, our team works together to support your mental and physical health—across your whole life.

children.text:
We’ve all, unfortunately, heard horror stories about IUD procedures. So at Tia, we offer IUD + Acupuncture to make your experience less *freaky* and more *comfy*.

This is just one of the ways we use multiple-tools-in-the-
toolkit — combining traditional modern medicine with evidence-based wellness practices to create an experience that keeps your comfort levels high.

Why acupuncture?

Acupuncture balances the nervous, immune, and musculoskeletal systems.

When paired with IUD procedures, acupuncture reduces uterine cramping, anxiety, nerves, and pain (by releasing endorphins).

Acupuncture is a highly effective, non-invasive pain relief aid that
makes the process of getting and removing an IUD less painful, and lessens discomfort afterward.
Did you know?

Acupuncture has been used for centuries to help reduce pain, both acute, and chronic. Its pain-relieving effects have been so powerful that in China, acupuncture has been successfully used in place of anesthesia during surgery!

main.shortDescription: We love this "set-it-and-forget-it" birth control method, but we know it can be painful to get inserted and removed. With acupuncture, reduce anxiety and pain before, during, and post procedure.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story. We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly
one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary gynecologist and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our model ensures your gynecology issues are never treated in isolation. Our providers are trained to connect the dots between symptoms like irregular periods or painful sex and your overall physical, mental, and emotional health. This allows us to offer holistic, trauma-informed care that gets to the root cause of conditions like painful sex, irregular bleeding, chronic pelvic pain . These symptoms often connect back to hormones or stress. Our providers don’t just prescribe birth control; we help you find the option that best fits your body, values, and lifestyle.
This approach helps women get comprehensive care, covering all aspects of their well-being:
Integrated Prevention:
After joining Tia, members complete
life-saving screenings at rates 2–3x the national average
, ensuring your gynecologic health (like Pap tests and cervical cancer screenings) is proactively managed.
Seamless Specialty Referrals:
If you need specialty care, Tia partners with top health systems to coordinate your referral while remaining your primary care home.
Mental Health is GYN Health
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression
because we recognize the deep connection between chronic pain, stress, and mental health.

Why Gynecological and Sexual Health matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Gynecological symptoms aren't isolated; they’re often signals from the rest of your body. A dedicated care team helps connect the dots, understanding how stress, sleep, and overall health can influence things like your period or pelvic pain, sexual wellness, or irregular bleeding.
Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously.

You're not imagining it. For too long, women's pain—from endometriosis to chronic pelvic issues—has been dismissed and minimized. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the full picture of women's reproductive and sexual health who will listen actively and never brush off your concerns
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health.

Building a relationship with a trusted primary care team means catching problems early, from getting your cervical cancer screening (Pap test) on time to addressing changes in your reproductive health , ensuring you stay healthier for the long term.

"There is no question too personal, too complicated, or too embarrassing when it comes to your gynecologic and sexual health. Our primary role is to be a compassionate, non-judgmental partner in your total well-being."
Rebecca Reilly, DNP, FNP-C
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for Sexual and Gynecological health
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
Seamlessly transition between virtual consults for birth control and in-person procedures like IUD insertion.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Your primary care provider is always in sync with your GYN care, so you never have to repeat your history to get a Pap test, STI screen, or tackle a more complex condition.
✓ Seamless Specialty Access
Tia partners with leading health systems to ensure that if you need specialty care—for complex issues like advanced endometriosis—you are referred and supported while Tia remains your home base.
✓ Holistic/comprehensive care
We use both medication and integrative options (like acupuncture or pelvic floor therapy referrals) for chronic conditions like PCOS or painful sex, aiming to get to the root and give you options.
✓ Care that grows with you
We are your health home for every stage—from your first Pap test to navigating menopause and beyond.

testimonials.Content: My experience at Tia was exceptional from start to finish. As someone with a 9–5 job, being able to book a 6 PM appointment for an OBGYN concern was a huge relief. From check-in, Angie made me feel welcomed and cared for, and Stephanie was professional, gentle with the blood draw, and made sure I felt comfortable the whole time. I saw Jamee, the nurse practitioner, who was incredibly supportive and knowledgeable. She took her time answering all my questions and helped me feel at ease with clear explanations and genuine care. Tia delivers warm, respectful, patient-focused care, and I’ve already started recommending it to friends and colleagues. Here, you truly feel heard and supported.
testimonials.Title: Clarissa B., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I had my IUD removed and replaced today which is probably one of the most uncomfortable procedures EVER but the care team made sure that my comfort was prioritized. In addition to the luxurious robe and beautiful interiors I've come to love from Tia, I was provided with a heating pad, white noise machine, and lavender essential oil to smell during my procedure. My NP was extremely thorough and asked if I had any questions and walked me through every step as it was happening. I felt very taken care of! As soon as I got home I was messaged aftercare information! It’s clear that Tia thinks through every step of their care and prioritizes comfort along with exceptional healthcare.
testimonials.Title: Chloe M., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: Amazing experience with Tia! The providers are compassionate and the office beautiful and efficient. The care is excellent with a focus on the whole person, offering mental health and hormone treatments as well as primary women's healthcare. Used acupuncture with my procedure and couldn't believe the difference it made. 10/10 recommend Tia to anyone
testimonials.Title: Alexis R.,Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I’ve read many reviews about this facility, but my experience was truly exceptional. I’ve had a lot of trauma around women’s health and avoided gynecologic care for years, but Tia completely changed that. Dr. Ryan Darvish is the first provider who made me feel genuinely safe and cared for. After an abnormal pap, I went through several difficult procedures, and he guided me through each one with compassion, clear communication, and a focus on keeping me comfortable. The same care and support were present during my IUD removal and replacement, which he made nearly painless. I’ve never felt so at ease during appointments that once felt overwhelming. Dr. Darvish is truly a gift, and I recommend him to anyone with fear or trauma around gynecologic care. Tia is now the only place I’ll go for my health needs.
testimonials.Title: Emily F., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I cannot say enough good things about my experience at Tia Clinic in Studio City, thanks to Jamee and Alauna. Their customer service was truly unmatched—every interaction was thoughtful, kind, and professional. Jamee was extremely patient with me, taking the time to answer every single one of my many questions without hesitation. She made me feel heard and valued, which is so rare these days. Both Jamee and Alauna went above and beyond to make me feel comfortable throughout my visit.  I’ve officially found my gyno for life with Jamee, and I couldn’t be more grateful for the care and support I received. If you’re looking for a team that genuinely cares about their patients, look no further than Tia Clinic.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Your First Step to Real Relief From Cramps & Pelvic Pain

Too many women are conditioned to believe that debilitating cramps and chronic pelvic pain are "just part of the deal." That's simply not true. At Tia, we know this pain seriously cramps your lifestyle, and we're here to flip the script. We'll start with a whole-person assessment to move past symptom suppression, find the root cause, and create a Care Plan that brings you lasting relief. You don't have to suffer in silence—let's find some answers.
Same day or next-day appointments may be available
Already a Tia member? Book here
Common Symptoms We Treat:
Severe menstrual pain
Pain during sex (dyspareunia)
Bleeding or spotting between periods
Chronic pelvic discomfort
Bloating, gas, constipation, or diarrhea related to your cycle
Potential Causes of Your Cramps or Pelvic Pain:
Endometriosis
PCOS (Polycystic Ovary Syndrome)
Urinary Tract Infections (UTIs)
Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS)
Ovarian Cysts
Pelvic Inflammatory Disease (PID)
Sexually Transmitted Infections (also known as STDs)

We know your experience with chronic pelvic pain or intense cramps can be exhausting. Because these symptoms often involve your reproductive, digestive, and hormonal systems, our comprehensive approach looks at all the possible connections to get to treat the whole you—not just the quick fix.
How the Cramps or Pelvic Pain Appointment Can Help
A Holistic Investigation:

We don't settle for "normal period pain." We connect the dots between your cycles, gut health, stress, and mood to find the root cause of your pain, whether it's endometriosis, IBS, or something else.
Real, Personalized Relief Options:
Get a customized Care Plan that goes beyond an over-the-counter fix. Options include hormonal therapy (like birth control), specific lifestyle changes, acupuncture, and medication management.
No More Dismissal:
Finally, discuss sensitive issues like painful sex or chronic discomfort with a provider who takes your symptoms seriously—even when they've been dismissed elsewhere.
The Tia Difference:
1. We treat you, not just the symptom
We recognize how pain affects your sleep, mood, and mental health, ensuring your Care Plan addresses the full picture.
2. We dig deeper than "normal period pain"
We're highly trained primary care providers who are experts in women's gynecological health and complex conditions like endometriosis and PCOS.
3. We offer integrative treatment options all in one place.
Your plan can include prescriptions, in-clinic procedures (like IUD insertion/removal), and integrative treatments like acupuncture and nutrition support.
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?
Yes! Tia will bill your insurance for your visit. Just like at any doctor’s office, you may be responsible for a co-pay or coinsurance, depending on your health plan
If not, what is out of pocket cost?
We are clear and transparent about pricing. Out-of-pocket costs are
detailed here
. Tia also offers Predictable Payments so cost is not a barrier to your health.
More accessible care with capped, predictable payments
We cap our monthly billing until your balance is paid off to help make women's healthcare more accessible, affordable, and fear-free.

children.text: Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Experiencing PMS, Irregular Periods, Abnormal Bleeding and Spotting?

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Same day or next-day appointments may be available
Already a Tia member? Book here
Common Symptoms We Treat:
Cycles that are less than 21 days or greater than 35 days
Bleeding through a tampon in under 2 hours
Bleeding that happens between periods (intermenstrual bleeding)
Bleeding after sex
Skipping more than 2 periods in a row and you are NOT pregnant
Postmenopausal bleeding
Potential causes of Abnormal Bleeding:
Hormonal imbalances
Uterine fibroids or polyps
Endometrial hyperplasia
Underlying disorders (e.g., thyroid conditions)
Blood clotting disorders
Stress, travel, or illness

Abnormal bleeding can be confusing because factors like sleep, stress, and illness can temporarily change your cycle. At Tia, we take time to review your full cycle history and lifestyle context to connect the dots and determine if your bleeding is related to a temporary stressor or something else.
How the Abnormal Bleeding appointment can help
Thorough Cycle Review:
Your Tia provider will conduct a thorough review of your medical history, current medications, and cycle history.
Lifestyle Connection:
Together we’ll explore any lifestyle, dietary, or other life changes that could be contributing to your symptoms.
Testing
: Additional testing, such as ultrasound and/or blood work, may be recommended to aid in reaching a diagnosis.
Personalized Treatment:

Your Care Plan is tailored to your specific diagnosis and preferences, ranging from hormonal medication (like birth control), lifestyle changes, to treating an underlying disorder or referring to a specialist if needed.
The Tia Difference:
1. We build a full picture
We integrate cycle history, medication review, and lifestyle or life changes to get to the root cause of the bleeding, ensuring a comprehensive diagnosis.
2. We take your story seriously
We listen without judgment and validate your experience, providing an honest space to discuss symptoms that may have been dismissed elsewhere.
3. We offer holistic solutions
We approach treatment with a blend of medical intervention and lifestyle support, depending on the underlying diagnosis.

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. Already a Tia member? Book here
Common Symptoms We Treat:
Changes in the color, odor, or texture of your discharge
Chronic or recurring itching and irritation in your vagina or vulva
Burning sensation, especially during sex or when urinating
New swelling or increased redness of the vulva
Vaginal pain and soreness
Symptoms of a
UTI (Urinary Tract Infection)
Potential Causes of Your Vaginal, Vulvar, or Urinary Symptoms:
Bacterial Vaginosis (BV)
Vaginal Candidiasis (Yeast Infection)
Urinary Tract Infections (UTIs)
Changes in vaginal pH or microbiome imbalance
Sexually Transmitted Infections (STIs)
Allergic reaction to hygiene products or detergent

We understand that these symptoms, even common ones like BV (which affects 29% of women aged 15-44), can be disruptive and hard to talk about. We create a compassionate space where you can openly discuss these concerns and get a plan tailored to you.
How the Vaginal, Vulvar, or Urinary Symptoms Appointment Can Help
A Holistic Evaluation:
We'll look at your symptoms, lifestyle, diet, and history of recurrence to find the root cause, often addressing imbalances beyond simple infection.
Flexible Care Options:
Get relief via virtual visits for typical symptoms, or schedule an in-person appointment.
Integrated Prevention & Treatment:
Receive a comprehensive plan that may include prescriptions, like antifungal or antibiotic medications, alongside lifestyle adjustments and integrative tools for long-term prevention.
Proactive Screening & Education:

We help you identify abnormal variations in discharge and common infection causes—from non-cotton underwear to laundry detergent or scented products—so you can maintain optimal health (as you define it!)
The Tia Difference:
1. We move beyond quick fixes
We don't just treat the flare-up; we assess root causes like dietary factors (saturated fat, fiber intake) and vitamin deficiencies (Vitamin D) to help prevent recurrence.
2. We offer an integrated toolkit:
We provide both modern prescriptions (oral or topical) and trusted integrative options, such as Boric acid suppositories and supportive Chinese herbal washes (Yin Care).
3. We offer non-judgmental expertise:
We provide a safe, honest space to discuss concerns like pain during sex or chronic infections without ever feeling embarrassed or dismissed.

A visit for these concerns helps uncover what’s driving issues like itching, burning, discharge, pain, or urinary discomfort. children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story. We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly
one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary gynecologist and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our model ensures your gynecology issues are never treated in isolation. Our providers are trained to connect the dots between symptoms like irregular periods or painful sex and your overall physical, mental, and emotional health. This allows us to offer holistic, trauma-informed care that gets to the root cause of conditions like painful sex, irregular bleeding, chronic pelvic pain . These symptoms often connect back to hormones or stress. Our providers don’t just prescribe birth control; we help you find the option that best fits your body, values, and lifestyle.
This approach helps women get comprehensive care, covering all aspects of their well-being:
Integrated Prevention:
After joining Tia, members complete
life-saving screenings at rates 2–3x the national average
, ensuring your gynecologic health (like Pap tests and cervical cancer screenings) is proactively managed.
Seamless Specialty Referrals:
If you need specialty care, Tia partners with top health systems to coordinate your referral while remaining your primary care home.
Mental Health is GYN Health
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression
because we recognize the deep connection between chronic pain, stress, and mental health.

Why Gynecological and Sexual Health matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Gynecological symptoms aren't isolated; they’re often signals from the rest of your body. A dedicated care team helps connect the dots, understanding how stress, sleep, and overall health can influence things like your period or pelvic pain, sexual wellness, or irregular bleeding.
Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously.

You're not imagining it. For too long, women's pain—from endometriosis to chronic pelvic issues—has been dismissed and minimized. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the full picture of women's reproductive and sexual health who will listen actively and never brush off your concerns
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health.

Building a relationship with a trusted primary care team means catching problems early, from getting your cervical cancer screening (Pap test) on time to addressing changes in your reproductive health , ensuring you stay healthier for the long term.

"There is no question too personal, too complicated, or too embarrassing when it comes to your gynecologic and sexual health. Our primary role is to be a compassionate, non-judgmental partner in your total well-being."
Rebecca Reilly, DNP, FNP-C
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for Sexual and Gynecological health
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
Seamlessly transition between virtual consults for birth control and in-person procedures like IUD insertion.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Your primary care provider is always in sync with your GYN care, so you never have to repeat your history to get a Pap test, STI screen, or tackle a more complex condition.
✓ Seamless Specialty Access
Tia partners with leading health systems to ensure that if you need specialty care—for complex issues like advanced endometriosis—you are referred and supported while Tia remains your home base.
✓ Holistic/comprehensive care
We use both medication and integrative options (like acupuncture or pelvic floor therapy referrals) for chronic conditions like PCOS or painful sex, aiming to get to the root and give you options.
✓ Care that grows with you
We are your health home for every stage—from your first Pap test to navigating menopause and beyond.

testimonials.Content: My experience at Tia was exceptional from start to finish. As someone with a 9–5 job, being able to book a 6 PM appointment for an OBGYN concern was a huge relief. From check-in, Angie made me feel welcomed and cared for, and Stephanie was professional, gentle with the blood draw, and made sure I felt comfortable the whole time. I saw Jamee, the nurse practitioner, who was incredibly supportive and knowledgeable. She took her time answering all my questions and helped me feel at ease with clear explanations and genuine care. Tia delivers warm, respectful, patient-focused care, and I’ve already started recommending it to friends and colleagues. Here, you truly feel heard and supported.
testimonials.Title: Clarissa B., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I had my IUD removed and replaced today which is probably one of the most uncomfortable procedures EVER but the care team made sure that my comfort was prioritized. In addition to the luxurious robe and beautiful interiors I've come to love from Tia, I was provided with a heating pad, white noise machine, and lavender essential oil to smell during my procedure. My NP was extremely thorough and asked if I had any questions and walked me through every step as it was happening. I felt very taken care of! As soon as I got home I was messaged aftercare information! It’s clear that Tia thinks through every step of their care and prioritizes comfort along with exceptional healthcare.
testimonials.Title: Chloe M., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: Amazing experience with Tia! The providers are compassionate and the office beautiful and efficient. The care is excellent with a focus on the whole person, offering mental health and hormone treatments as well as primary women's healthcare. Used acupuncture with my procedure and couldn't believe the difference it made. 10/10 recommend Tia to anyone
testimonials.Title: Alexis R.,Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I’ve read many reviews about this facility, but my experience was truly exceptional. I’ve had a lot of trauma around women’s health and avoided gynecologic care for years, but Tia completely changed that. Dr. Ryan Darvish is the first provider who made me feel genuinely safe and cared for. After an abnormal pap, I went through several difficult procedures, and he guided me through each one with compassion, clear communication, and a focus on keeping me comfortable. The same care and support were present during my IUD removal and replacement, which he made nearly painless. I’ve never felt so at ease during appointments that once felt overwhelming. Dr. Darvish is truly a gift, and I recommend him to anyone with fear or trauma around gynecologic care. Tia is now the only place I’ll go for my health needs.
testimonials.Title: Emily F., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I cannot say enough good things about my experience at Tia Clinic in Studio City, thanks to Jamee and Alauna. Their customer service was truly unmatched—every interaction was thoughtful, kind, and professional. Jamee was extremely patient with me, taking the time to answer every single one of my many questions without hesitation. She made me feel heard and valued, which is so rare these days. Both Jamee and Alauna went above and beyond to make me feel comfortable throughout my visit.  I’ve officially found my gyno for life with Jamee, and I couldn’t be more grateful for the care and support I received. If you’re looking for a team that genuinely cares about their patients, look no further than Tia Clinic.
testimonials.Title: Tia H., Tia Patient

Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for primary care
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
In-person and virtual care, so you can choose what works for you. Visit our clinics for hands-on, welcoming care, or schedule virtual visits for quick check-ups or prescription refills from home.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Every provider you see at Tia works together from a single health record and shared Care Plan, so your care stays connected, not fragmented.
✓ Holistic and comprehensive care
We treat your health as one connected system — integrating mind, body, and hormones to uncover the “why” behind your symptoms and help you feel well from every angle.
✓ Care that grows with you
No need to switch practices as your needs change. Your team supports you though every life stage and health need.

children.text: The Problem

Mental healthcare can feel fragmented, separating your mind from your body. When you feel overwhelmed, anxious, or like "something is off," you may be told it's "just stress" without a look at your hormones, sleep, digestion, or other causes. This leaves you managing symptoms without understanding
why
you feel this way, especially problematic for women who face disproportionately high rates of anxiety and depression compared to men.

Tia’s Solution for Women

Our mental health care starts with treating your body, not just your brain. Our expert-led whole-person approach looks at the full picture of your health—including the physiological effects of stress, hormones, and sleep—to find the root cause. You get a connected, relationship-based approach with an expanded toolkit, including care from specialty trained primary care providers and psychiatric specialists if needed. We consider mental health a core part of your physical health, ensuring consistent, holistic, and long-term support.

Feeling anxious or overwhelmed all the time
Noticing symptoms of depression or sadness that won't lift
Feeling chronic stress, burnout, or irritability
Struggling with sleep issues or brain fog that are affecting your daily life
Experiencing hormone-related mood changes or feeling "off" after a life stage like postpartum
Navigating a challenging life stage

Why Mental Health matters for women
The Connection is Read: Your Mind
is
Your Body

You deserve to feel like yourself again. The belief that mental health and physical health are separate has led to a fragmented system where patients are often left to piece together care on their own. This is frustrating, especially since the impact of mental health on chronic conditions like heart disease, diabetes, and stroke is significant and preventable. Our model is designed to support you by acknowledging that your mental, physical, and reproductive health are inextricably linked.
When “Just Stressed” Gets Overlooked
Women experience anxiety and depression at nearly
twice

the rate of men, yet stress and mood changes are often hard to fully assess in today’s fragmented healthcare system. Key contributors, like hormones, sleep, pain, and life context, can go unrecognized, especially when national depression screening rates in primary care hover around 39%.
Our integrated approach is designed to make it easier to catch what’s really going on. That’s why over
of our patients receive evidence-based depression screening.
A Model Build for the Long Term

Mental health care shouldn't be limited to just one solution. Tia's integrated model is designed to empower you with personalized care, offering a full suite of options from clinical to subclinical modalities—like medication, acupuncture, or supplements. By connecting your primary care with specialized psychiatric support when needed, we ensure you have a collaborative Care Team providing a personalized path toward feeling like your whole self again.e.

“We're here to support our patients in making the decisions that are right for them. Whatever you’re going through, I want you to know: Tia is here to support you feeling better—physically and emotionally. Because improved mental health is improved health.”
Jessica Horwitz, Chief Clinical Officer
How Tia is different for Mental Health
Tia's approach treats your mental well-being as a core part of your physical health, not something separate.
✓ We Connect Mind, Body, and Hormones

Our providers are specialty trained to look at the whole-system effects of stress across your sleep, digestion, hormones, and more. To ensure we get to the root of your experience, together we may consider additional labs and testing for your unique situation.
✓ An Expanded, Expert-Led Toolkit

You get comprehensive care from specialty trained primary care providers, backed by psychiatric mental health nurse practitioners as needed. Your plan offers a wide range of solutions beyond medication—from acupuncture and supplements to holistic planning and nutrition counseling.
✓ Support Designed for Women's Life Stages

We understand that anxiety and depression rates are disproportionately high for women and often surface during major life transitions. We offer care for women navigating hormonal shifts, postpartum recovery, and perimenopause, providing consistent follow-up and long-term support.

Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: The Problem

Feeling confused or judged about your experience with weight and metabolism is a common but frustrating experience. In fact, ~60% of patients report experiencing bias from doctors. When care focuses only on the scale or offers quick fixes, it often fails to look at the full picture of your physical, emotional, and metabolic health. With the rise of prescription solutions like GLP-1s, many women are also left wondering what is right for their body and how to navigate side effects or long-term management. This fragmented approach leaves you feeling stuck and like you've tried everything.

Tia’s Solution
Tia's Weight & Metabolic Health care is built to understand your whole body, not just a number. Our providers are trained on a Health at Every Size (HAES) approach , meaning the goal is helping
you
feel well, not fit someone else's idea of wellness. We create a personalized, non-judgmental Care Plan, integrating all factors from hormones and sleep to stress and nutrition for long-term support.

Experiencing unexplained weight gain or weight loss
Feeling stuck in current routines and like you’ve “tried everything” to manage your weight
Curious about or seeking GLP-1s, other weight management medications, or non-rx weight management solutions
Needing help managing chronic conditions like diabetes or high blood pressure.
